name: gaiji_double_paren

meta.note: [provenance:reference-impl-aligned §6.4 + AOZORA-ANNOTATION external_character.html + JIS X 0213 2026-06-30] The corpus form ※［＃二重かっこ開く］ / ※［＃二重かっこ閉じ］ (32 occurrences each): a bare, unquoted, addressless gaiji reference whose description names a known dictionary glyph — 始め/終わり二重括弧 → U+FF5F ｟ / U+FF60 ｠ (FULLWIDTH LEFT/RIGHT WHITE PARENTHESIS). Recognised precisely because the description is a dictionary entry, so an ordinary ［＃…］ directive is not mistaken for a gaiji. The canonical serialization quotes the description; the resolved glyph round-trips to its quoted reference form, never the bare glyph.

source: 彼は※［＃二重かっこ開く］愛※［＃二重かっこ閉じ］と書いた

expected.html: 彼は｟愛｠と書いた
expected.serialize: 彼は※［＃「二重かっこ開く」］愛※［＃「二重かっこ閉じ」］と書いた
